question_text: A distance-time graph shows a line with a steep gradient followed by a gentle gradient. What does this represent?
correct_answer: The object was moving quickly at first (steep gradient = high speed) and then slowed down (gentle gradient = lower speed).
explanation: On a distance-time graph, the gradient (steepness) represents speed. A steeper line means faster speed, and a gentler line means slower speed. This graph shows the object decelerating.
